title: macOS App replacing existing signature
description: Practical steps to replace application signatures on macOS to fix Gatekeeper and dependency library signature issues.
content:
## Background

I occasionally download genuine software for testing purposes during daily use, so I used some methods to solve certain problems.

The following content is for testing purposes only; it is recommended that you purchase genuine software.

## Enabling

### Problem 1

After downloading a certain software, upon opening it, the message "is damaged and can’t be opened. You should move it to the Trash." appears.

What is the cause of this problem?

This is actually a macOS Gatekeeper issue. Try these steps: To resolute Gatekeeper issues on macOS Sierra, you might have to partially or completely disable Gatekeeper checks.

Solution

```plaintext
Option I
For a certain application run in Terminal:
sudo xattr -rd com.apple.quarantine /Applications/[LockedApp].app
Option II
To disable checks globally run in Terminal:
sudo spctl --master-disable
```After resolving the issue, the app can launch.

### Problem 2

If it launches normally, this problem should not occur.

Specifically:

```console
Exception Type:        EXC_CRASH (SIGABRT)
Exception Codes:       0x0000000000000000, 0x0000000000000000
Exception Note:        EXC_CORPSE_NOTIFY

Termination Reason:    DYLD, [0x5] Code Signature

Application Specific Information:
dyld: launch, loading dependent libraries

Dyld Error Message: *****

```The application crashed.

The error message indicates a dynamic library signing issue. The solution is to resolve the signing problem. Follow the macOS instructions to re-sign the app.

1. Open the Keychain Access tool.

2. Create a certificate.

![Create a certificate](/assets/img/post/2019-12-25-AppReplacingExistingSignature1.png)

3. Set the certificate name.

![Setting the certificate name](/assets/img/post/2019-12-25-AppReplacingExistingSignature2.png)

4. Create.

5. Open a bash command, as follows.

```bash

sudo codesign -f -s certificateName /Applications/yourApp.app

```This completes the process.

You can now open the app more often.
